title: Initial Cold Call Script
description: Opening script for first-time prospects who requested information
when_to_use: Use when calling leads who have expressed interest in final expense information through online forms, direct mail response, or referrals

script_blocks.label: Warm Opening
script_blocks.content:
Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] with [Company]. How are you doing today?

[PAUSE FOR RESPONSE]

I'm calling because you recently expressed interest in learning about final expense insurance. Is this still something you'd like information about?

[PAUSE FOR CONFIRMATION]

script_blocks.label: Getting Permission
script_blocks.content:
Great! I appreciate your time today. Before I share some information with you, let me ask - what prompted you to look into this type of coverage?

[LISTEN TO THEIR RESPONSE]

I understand. Many people in your situation are looking for a way to make sure their family isn't burdened with funeral costs. That's exactly what final expense insurance is designed for.

script_blocks.label: Time Commitment
script_blocks.content:
Would you have about 10 minutes for me to explain how this works and see if it might be a good fit for your situation?

[IF YES: Continue to needs discovery]
[IF BUSY: 'I completely understand. When would be a better time for you? I can call back at your convenience.']

tips: Use their name within the first 30 seconds to create familiarity
Pause after asking if they're still interested - let them confirm
Listen carefully to their motivation and reference it throughout the call
Match their energy level and pace of speaking
If they say they're busy, always offer to reschedule rather than rush

common_responses.handling: I understand - may I ask what changed? Sometimes people think this is more complicated or expensive than it actually is.

common_responses.response: How did you get my number?
common_responses.handling: You filled out a form requesting information about final expense insurance. I'm following up to provide that information.
I'm calling to provide the information you requested about final expense insurance. My job is to help you understand how it works so you can decide if it's right for your situation.

title: Professional Voicemail Script
description: Compelling voicemail message that encourages callbacks
when_to_use: When prospect doesn't answer your call - designed to encourage callback rather than sound sales-y

script_blocks.label: Clear Identification
script_blocks.content: Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] with [Company Name].

script_blocks.label: Reason for Call
script_blocks.content: I'm calling about the final expense information you requested. I have some good news about coverage options that might work well for your situation.

script_blocks.label: Callback Information
script_blocks.content: My direct number is [Phone Number]. I'll try you again tomorrow, but feel free to call me back at your convenience.

script_blocks.label: Professional Closing
script_blocks.content: Again, that's [Your Name] at [Phone Number]. Have a great day!

tips: Keep it under 30 seconds total
Mention something specific to their inquiry to show legitimacy
Give your phone number twice - once fast, once slow
Sound upbeat and helpful, not desperate or pushy
Don't oversell in the voicemail - save details for conversation
Always mention you'll try again so they know you're persistent but professional

variations.content: Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] following up about the final expense coverage we discussed. I wanted to make sure you had all the information you needed to make a decision. Please give me a call at [number] when you have a few minutes.

Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] with [Company]. I have the final expense information you requested and wanted to go over a few options that might work for your budget. Please call me back at [number] when it's convenient.

title: Follow-Up Text Message
description: Professional text for prospects who haven't responded to calls
when_to_use: 24-48 hours after initial contact attempt, and only if they've opted in to texting or provided a mobile number

script_blocks.label: Reference Previous Contact
script_blocks.content: I called about your final expense insurance inquiry. I found some great options that could work for your budget.

script_blocks.label: Give Them Options
script_blocks.content: Would you prefer I call you back or would you like to call me at [Phone Number]?

tips: Only text if they've given permission or it's a mobile number they provided
Keep it brief and professional - this isn't a social media message
Give them options for how to respond (call back vs. call you)
Don't text multiple times without a response - it becomes spam
Always include your phone number for easy callback
Use proper grammar and spelling - you're representing your company

follow_up_sequence.message: Hi [Name], wanted to follow up on final expense coverage. Any questions I can answer? [Phone number]
Hi [Name], checking in about final expense insurance. Still interested in learning more? [Phone number]

title: Warm Lead Script
description: Script for leads from referrals or previous customers
when_to_use: When calling prospects who were referred by existing clients or who have some connection to your business

script_blocks.label: Referral Opening
script_blocks.content: Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] with [Company]. [Referrer Name] suggested I give you a call about final expense insurance. How are you doing today?

script_blocks.label: Establish Connection
script_blocks.content: [Referrer Name] mentioned that you might be interested in learning about protecting your family from funeral costs. Is that something you've been thinking about?

script_blocks.label: Get Permission
script_blocks.content: I helped [Referrer Name] get coverage that gives them real peace of mind. Would you like me to explain how it works and see if something similar might make sense for your situation?

tips: Always mention the referrer's name early in the conversation
Reference what you did for the referrer to build credibility
Ask permission before launching into your presentation
Use the referrer's satisfaction as social proof
Be prepared to answer questions about the referrer's experience
referrer_phrases: I just helped [Name] get $15,000 of coverage for about $85 per month
[Name] was so happy with their coverage that they suggested I call you
[Name] mentioned you might have similar concerns about protecting your family
After I helped [Name] with their final expense coverage, they thought you might be interested

title: Appointment Setting Script
description: Script for scheduling formal presentations rather than selling on first call
when_to_use: When leads prefer appointments, when dealing with couples, or when initial call timing isn't ideal for full presentation

script_blocks.label: Professional Opening
script_blocks.content: Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] with [Company]. I'm calling about the final expense information you requested. Do you have just a quick minute?

script_blocks.label: Quick Overview
script_blocks.content: I specialize in helping people your age protect their families from funeral costs with affordable final expense insurance. Rather than try to explain everything over the phone right now, I'd prefer to schedule a time when I can give you my full attention and answer all your questions properly.

script_blocks.label: Schedule Request
script_blocks.content: Would you prefer a morning or afternoon appointment? I have some time available [Day] at [Time] or [Day] at [Time]. Which works better for you?

tips: Keep the initial call short - just enough to build interest
Position the appointment as benefiting them (full attention, detailed answers)
Give specific time options rather than asking when they're available
Confirm the appointment with date, time, and your phone number
Follow up with a text or email confirmation
appointment_confirmation: Perfect! I have you down for [Day] at [Time].
I'll call you at [Phone Number] - is that still the best number?
The conversation usually takes about 15-20 minutes.
I'll send you a quick text reminder the day before.
Do you have any initial questions I can answer when we talk?
